frameworks.name: sensory recruitment framework
frameworks.one_line: Visual working memory storage recruits sensory cortex representations.

frameworks.name: Xu (2020) critique of sensory recruitment
frameworks.one_line: Verbal-model critique arguing parietal cortex is central to VWM.

frameworks.name: formal quantitative models of VWM
frameworks.one_line: Quantitative model-based accounts contrasted with verbal/intuitive accounts.

core_question: Does Xu's (2020) commentary pose a serious challenge to the sensory recruitment framework of visual working memory?
key_assumptions: Both behavioural and neural evidence must constrain VWM theories
Formal quantitative models should be preferred over verbal/intuitive ones
Critiques must accurately characterise the framework they target
key_findings: Xu's commentary mischaracterises the sensory recruitment framework
Xu's commentary favours verbal models and intuition over formal quantitative models
Xu's interpretation of the cited literature is not even-handed
interpretations: Xu (2020) does not pose a serious challenge to the sensory recruitment framework
Sensory recruitment can be incorporated into a broader working model of VWM spanning occipital and parietal contributions
